reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: All 6 gates passed. Spec cross-reference: outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json requires 'data_column_sidecars_by_range sidecars must be sent in (slot, column_index) order' with assertion 'forall resp, i < j: resp[i].slot < resp[j].slot || (resp[i].slot == resp[j].slot && resp[i].col < resp[j].col)'. Code verification confirms Phase 03's analysis: availableColumns is constructed via filter() at dataColumnResponseValidation.ts:75, preserving request order; getManyBinary() at abstractPrefixedRepository.ts:89-95 preserves input array order; handler yields sidecars at dataColumnSidecarsByRange.ts:35-44 and 83-92 in the order returned by getManyBinary, without sorting by column_index. Exploitability: An attacker can trigger this via P2P ReqResp by sending a DataColumnSidecarsByRangeRequest with columns=[5,2,8], causing the responder to yield sidecars in request order (5,2,8) rather than ascending column_index order (2,5,8), violating the spec requirement. Impact is limited to interoperability failures with strict clients that enforce ordering—does not cause consensus splits, validator slashing, or network-wide disruption. Severity remains Low per BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json thresholds (minor protocol compliance violation affecting <0.01% of network). Verdict: CONFIRMED_POTENTIAL due to uncertain real-world impact on client interoperability.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: data_column_sidecars_by_range sidecars must be sent in (slot, column_index) order.
